title: ambrosia pannosa
intro: Ambrosia pannosa, also known as Hoary Puccoon, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its yellow, fragrant flowers and hairy leaves. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Ambrosia pannosa is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used topically as a poultice to treat skin conditions and wounds.
